name: Nidhogg
description: Nidhogg, also Nidhoggr (Old Norse Níðhöggr, 'Malice Striker' or 'Curse Striker'), is a dragon or serpent who gnaws at a root of the world tree Yggdrasil. In Norse cosmology, Yggdrasil is the central pillar of the cosmos, connecting the nine realms. Nidhogg resides beneath Yggdrasil in Niflheimr, the realm of primordial ice and darkness. He is a monstrous figure, a symbol of decay, destruction, and the relentless cycle of cosmic renewal. Nidhogg is not merely a destructive force; his actions contribute to the eventual end of the world, Ragnarok. He chews on the roots of Yggdrasil, constantly threatening to destabilize the entire cosmic structure. In some accounts, he carries the corpses of oathbreakers and murderers in his wings, further emphasizing his association with evil and the underworld. His existence underscores the perpetual struggle between order and chaos in the Norse universe, a constant reminder of the impermanence of even the most fundamental structures.
subtitle: The Root-Gnawing Dragon of Niflheim
appearance: Nidhogg is described as a massive, serpentine dragon with dark scales and piercing red eyes. His body is covered in jagged spines and bony protrusions, and his teeth are sharp and capable of tearing through the toughest materials, including the roots of Yggdrasil. He is often depicted as being surrounded by a miasma of decay and death, and his presence exudes an aura of dread.
habitat: Nidhogg dwells in Niflheimr, specifically beneath one of the roots of Yggdrasil. This root is located near the spring Hvergelmir, which is a source of many rivers and is associated with the underworld.
behavior: Nidhogg is driven by a primal instinct to destroy and undermine the cosmic order. He relentlessly gnaws at the roots of Yggdrasil, seeking to bring about its collapse and the ensuing Ragnarok. He is also said to torment the souls of the wicked, carrying them in his wings and dragging them down to the depths of Niflheimr. He is a symbol of malice, vengeance, and the inescapable nature of decay.
weaknesses: The strength and resilience of Yggdrasil

keyMyths.title: Gnawing at Yggdrasil
keyMyths.description: Nidhogg relentlessly gnaws at the roots of Yggdrasil, the world tree, threatening to bring about the end of the world. This action symbolizes the constant struggle between chaos and order in Norse cosmology.

keyMyths.title: Carrying the Dead
keyMyths.description: Nidhogg carries the corpses of oathbreakers and murderers in his wings, transporting them to the depths of Niflheimr. This reinforces his association with evil and the punishment of the wicked in the afterlife.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A comprehensive source for Norse mythology, detailing the creation of the world, the gods, and various mythical creatures, including Nidhogg.
sources.text: A comprehensive source for Norse mythology, detailing the creation of the world, the gods, and various mythical creatures, including Nidhogg.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Norse mythology and cosmology, offering glimpses of Nidhogg's role in the cosmic drama.
